$comment: Five injection strategies × happy-path. Each case fixes the (skill request, credential, upstream base, strategy params) and pins the byte-exact upstream request the proxy MUST produce. A conformant impl reaches the same headers / url / body for every case. All credential values here are fake (FAKE / FAKEDEMO patterns) — secret scanners flagging this corpus are over-fitting.

vectors.name: openai_chat_completion
vectors.description: authorizationBearer: OpenAI chat-completion call. Adds Authorization: Bearer <secret> alongside the skill's Content-Type. Existing skill headers preserved.

vectors.name: legacy_basic_oauth1ish
vectors.description: authorizationBasic: older OAuth1-style service that wants Basic auth. Credential is the password half; user comes from strategy_params.basic_user. Empty body (GET) is preserved.

vectors.name: anthropic_messages
vectors.description: headerNamed: Anthropic's x-api-key. The proxy adds the named header verbatim — case preserved, not URL-encoded. Existing skill-supplied headers of the same name would be overwritten (vault-injected value wins); this case shows two distinct headers staying side-by-side.

vectors.name: legacy_google_apikey
vectors.description: queryParam: legacy Google-API-style ?key=<secret>. The skill's existing query string (address=...) is preserved; the credential param is appended with '&'. Credential is URL-encoded per RFC 3986 — special chars (&, =) MUST be percent-encoded so they don't break parsing.

vectors.name: oauth2_client_credentials
vectors.description: bodyField: OAuth2 client_credentials grant. The proxy parses the JSON body, sets the credential at the named JSONPath ('client_secret'), and re-serializes with sort_keys=True + separators=(',', ':') for byte stability. Existing skill body fields are preserved; the credential field is added (or overwritten if present).
